headlineSummary: OpenAI drew the clearest negative reaction on trust and model-quality complaints, while Anthropic was mixed to slightly negative with one praise and one comparison complaint, based on limited evidence [E2][E3][E1].

ranking.summary: OpenAI’s sentiment was negative, driven by a strong scam/trust criticism plus a model-comparison complaint about GPT-5.5 being worse than GPT-5.4 [E2][E3].
Anthropic landed in mixed neutral territory: one comment praised Claude Code as very fun, but another said Opus-4.7 was slightly worse than Opus-4.6, so the evidence is limited and split [E1][E3].

evidence.excerpt: These have been one of the most fun times using a computer in a long time. Claude Code + Godot = fun. Going back to it.

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly names Claude Code and strongly praises the experience using it for game development as highly fun and worthwhile.

evidence.excerpt: This program is a complete scam. Even if 100 people find "bugs", they will only pay out to one person.

annotations.rationale: In the context of the OpenAI GPT-5.5 bio bug bounty story, the commenter calls the program a 'complete scam' and criticizes its payout structure.

evidence.excerpt: Curiously, gpt-5.5 is noticeably worse than gpt-5.4, and opus-4.7 is slightly worse than opus-4.6.

annotations.rationale: The excerpt explicitly references GPT-5.5 and says it performs worse than GPT-5.4, indicating mild negative sentiment about OpenAI's newer model.
The excerpt explicitly references Opus-4.7 and says it is slightly worse than Opus-4.6, indicating mild negative sentiment about Anthropic's newer model.
